executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: The highest remaining weakness is still flat marketing outcome movement; the main new runtime break was wrapper-layer false negatives and verifier drift, both repaired in this run.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Convert marketing learning into measurable Codeberg adoption movement while preserving the repaired fail-closed verifier and wrapper-proof health boundaries.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json comparison with explicit live-vs-persisted separation

owns: bounded timeout reruns and verifier refreshes
wrapper-false-negative suppression for proven mirror health
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Marketing remains the highest-risk unresolved loop because outcome movement is still flat
findings.mechanism: The marketing audit still records distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the bottleneck, and the independent verifier still passes only with flat primary-repo movement explicitly kept open.
findings.root_cause: The marketing system is learning and reusing shared artifacts, but current tactics still have not produced measurable Codeberg adoption movement.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep replacing tactics and, if necessary, the marketing runtime itself until Codeberg movement becomes measurable instead of accepting activity-only health.

findings.title: Mirror sync health had a wrapper-layer false negative even though replication was healthy
findings.mechanism: The Gateway job reported an agent response failure, but the sync script completed successfully and remote heads were aligned; the failure lived in the wrapper/response layer, not in the replication path.
findings.root_cause: A lightweight owner-only operational job still depends on an agent response layer that can fail after the real sync work succeeds.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep health-monitor proof checks bound to underlying mirror state so wrapper-only failures do not masquerade as operational replication failures.

findings.title: Docs verifier drift briefly produced contradictory pass/fail evidence and was repaired in the same run
findings.mechanism: The docs agentic artifact had drifted to fail while the independent verifier markdown still said pass; rerunning the verifier restored a coherent independently verified pass artifact set.
findings.root_cause: Verifier freshness had fallen behind newer docs-loop evidence.
findings.recommended_fix: Preserve the fail-closed artifact checks and rerun the independent verifier whenever docs-loop evidence moves underneath the last signoff.

findings.title: Persisted disabled cron history still exists and must stay separated from live runtime claims
findings.mechanism: jobs.json still contains three disabled legacy jobs, while live Gateway cron currently shows 20 jobs with 0 live-disabled.
findings.root_cause: Historical retention is intentional, but it becomes dangerous if any watchdog or verifier collapses persisted history back into live topology reporting.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep all live-topology checks bound to openclaw cron list --json and report persisted disabled history only in separate persisted-history fields.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Convert marketing self-improvement into measurable Codeberg adoption movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This is still the highest-risk unresolved system weakness after the verifier and wrapper-boundary repairs held green.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The system rewards real distribution outcomes instead of activity-only loop health.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep wrapper-false-negative suppression tied to underlying operational proof for mirror sync
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This run exposed a response-layer false negative on an owner-only operational loop.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Operational replication issues are judged by real remote state, not by a post-tool response failure.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Preserve fail-closed verifier freshness and live-vs-persisted topology gates
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Docs and architecture signoff drift reappeared quickly once runtime evidence moved.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Repair loops continue to need fresh external signoff before being treated as healthy.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Patched the health monitor to (a) verify real mirror state before treating the mirror-sync wrapper failure as a live outage and (b) rerun the docs verifier when docs-loop contract evidence drifts.
Patched the architecture verifier to fail closed on non-architecture health issues reported by the live health monitor instead of only checking independent-signoff freshness.
Reran the docs verifier, which refreshed the agentic review and restored a coherent independently verified pass artifact set.

independent_verification.summary: Fresh reruns now confirm green health-monitor status, repaired docs verifier coherence, fail-closed architecture signoff, clean live-vs-persisted topology separation, and machine-verifiable shared market-intelligence reuse.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing outcomes remain flat despite healthy loop discipline
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Codeberg adoption is still flat across the recent measurement window, so the active tactics are not yet producing measurable primary-repo movement.

mirror_wrapper_state.last_error: ⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response. Note: some tool actions may have already been executed — please verify before retrying.
